videos.title: Weight and Capacity Word Problems | Real Life Maths
videos.description: Solve fun word problems about weight and capacity! How much does your school bag weigh? How much water fills a bucket? Use grams, kilograms, millilitres, and litres to answer.
learningObjectives: Read and understand word problems about weight and capacity
Identify whether to add, subtract, or convert
keyConceptsCovered: Working with mixed units in problems

videos.title: Converting Grams to Kilograms and mL to Litres
videos.description: Review conversions needed for word problems. Convert between g-kg and mL-L so you can solve problems where units are mixed.
learningObjectives: Convert units before solving word problems
keyConceptsCovered: Convert to the same unit before adding or subtracting
Step-by-step approach to mixed unit problems

videos.title: kg-g and L-mL Conversion Practice | Step by Step
videos.description: Apply conversion skills to solve word problems. Practice adding 2 kg 300 g + 1 kg 700 g and finding how much juice is left after pouring.
learningObjectives: Add and subtract with mixed weight units
Add and subtract with mixed capacity units
keyConceptsCovered: Adding 2 kg 300 g + 1 kg 700 g = 4 kg
Subtracting 5 L - 2 L 500 mL = 2 L 500 mL
Carrying over: 800 g + 400 g = 1 kg 200 g

videos.title: Introduction to Weighing | Heavy and Light for Kids
videos.description: Revisit weighing basics and use estimation in word problems. Before calculating, guess the answer - is the result going to be more or less?
learningObjectives: Use common sense to check answers
keyConceptsCovered: Checking if an answer makes sense

videos.title: Measuring Capacity | Litres and Millilitres for Kids
videos.description: Challenge: Solve tricky capacity word problems. If a jug holds 2 L and you pour out 750 mL three times, how much is left? Think step by step!

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Full word problem coverage with challenge for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete word problem coverage with conversion review

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Conversion review first, then core word problems

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: Ravi buys 2 kg of apples and 500 g of grapes. What is the total weight?
questions.explanation: 2 kg of apples + 500 g of grapes = 2 kg 500 g (or 2500 g). We can write it as 2 kg 500 g.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Create word problems using Indian contexts: buying vegetables at the market, filling water bottles, measuring ingredients for cooking. Let children create their own word problems too.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Involve your child when cooking or shopping. Ask: We need 1 kg 500 g of potatoes but we already have 700 g. How much more do we need? This makes maths real and fun!
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Word problems are just real-life stories with numbers! Read the problem carefully, find the numbers and units, decide if you need to add or subtract, and solve step by step.
